task_completion.description: Juris-M application is open and the central library list view is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Juris-M application window displaying a list of library items in the main central pane.

task_completion.description: The default 'Title', 'Creator', and 'Date' columns are visible in the library list view header.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the column headers at the top of the main list pane. The headers 'Title', 'Creator', and 'Date' should be present.

task_completion.description: The 'Date Added' column is selected in the column customization menu or added to the header.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the column picker dropdown menu (accessed via the small icon at the top right of the list pane) with 'Date Added' checked, OR the 'Date Added' column newly appearing in the header.

task_completion.description: The 'Item Type' column is selected in the column customization menu or added to the header.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the column picker dropdown menu with 'Item Type' checked, OR the 'Item Type' column newly appearing in the header.

task_completion.description: The final list view header simultaneously displays all required columns: 'Title', 'Creator', 'Date', 'Date Added', and 'Item Type'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the final state of the column headers in the main list pane. All five required columns ('Title', 'Creator', 'Date', 'Date Added', 'Item Type') must be visible at the same time.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Juris-M GUI to customize the columns.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the column picker dropdown menu being clicked and interacted with, rather than terminal windows modifying the SQLite database directly.

integrity.description: The library contains the expected synthetic data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for historical US Supreme Court cases and law review articles (e.g., Brown v. Board of Education, Marbury v. Madison) in the library list view to confirm the genuine application and database are being used.

integrity.description: The agent did not remove the required existing columns.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure that 'Title', 'Creator', and 'Date' remain checked in the column picker and visible in the header throughout the process; they should not be replaced or removed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target columns correspond to internal IDs 'zotero-items-column-dateAdded' and 'zotero-items-column-itemType'. The Juris-M database is synthetically populated with 10 historical US Supreme Court cases and law review articles (e.g., Brown v. Board of Education, Marbury v. Madison). Look for these specific items in the library view to confirm the correct application state and database are being used.
